summary: Doc-only triage. Lane 2 Batches 1-3 are shipped (Today Continue, Write capture inbox, Train active/history). Compared the three remaining surfaces (Home protocols, Patterns, localization layout) and selected Home protocols, narrowed to active-run step progression. Patterns deferred (no concrete UI claim has changed). Localization layout deferred (translation intake still parked; without translated values, layout regression has nothing locale-distinctive to verify). Within Home protocols, narrowed further to step progression specifically; step skip, revert, archive, schedule-window status, and template editing remain deferred. Added Batch 4 decision memo to docs/workflows/ui-regression-plan.md. Queued the implementation slice owlory-ui-regression-batch-4-home-protocol-run-step-progression with explicit allowed_paths and required_validations.

contract_status_changes.before: Lane 2 Batches 1-3 shipped (Today Continue, Write capture inbox, Train active/history). docs/workflows/ui-regression-plan.md noted that no next surface had been selected; the original owlory-ui-regression-next-surface-triage slice (which selected Write and Train via two parallel agents) was already done. No Batch 4 candidate had been chosen.
contract_status_changes.after: Batch 4 surface explicitly selected: Home protocols, narrowed to active-run step progression. Decision memo recorded in docs/workflows/ui-regression-plan.md under '### Batch 4 decision' with a candidate comparison table and a Why-Home reasoning section. Implementation slice owlory-ui-regression-batch-4-home-protocol-run-step-progression queued with explicit scope (open active run sheet via Today Continue route, tap Complete on first pending step, assert step transitions out of pending), required infrastructure (home.protocolRun.step.<uuid> and home.protocolRun.step.action.complete.<uuid> identifiers, new XCUITest class, DOMAIN=home matrix branch), and proof_level target running-app-smoke.

residual_risks: Triage premise reconciliation: the incoming prompt described the *original* Batch 2 triage (Write vs Train) as if it had not yet been performed. The user confirmed the right interpretation was Batch 4 selection from remaining surfaces. A parallel agent running with a stale view could try the original triage again; the new slice ID owlory-ui-regression-batch-4-surface-triage prevents direct queue-record collision with the existing owlory-ui-regression-next-surface-triage.
Active-run step progression is one sub-behavior of Home protocols. Skip, revert, archive, template editing, and schedule-window status remain deferred and would need their own future scoped slices.

The implementation slice will add new accessibility identifiers (home.protocolRun.step.<uuid>, home.protocolRun.step.action.complete.<uuid>) on the same SessionCardView-equivalent that already carries the home.protocol.step.accessibility.* labels added in the prior Home action accessibility slice. Identifiers and labels coexist (different SwiftUI modifiers); reviewers should know both exist for the same view.
This slice does not prove anything about running-app behavior, screenshot integrity, device behavior, or TestFlight identity. proof_level is doc-only.
recommended_next_reason: Queued behind this triage with explicit scope and required validations. The supervisor dry-run already selects it as the next eligible slice.
